findings.message: The ADR defines two incompatible commit-hint predicates: Context/D1 say the hint is for “all scopes completed and at least one zero_findings”, but D5 makes the implementation condition `check-approved OK` plus `review.json` existence and D5.2 explicitly allows an all-Empty diff with zero zero_findings to emit the hint. That leaves implementers with conflicting requirements for whether `>=1 zero_findings` is mandatory; either D5 should include that predicate or the earlier decision text should be relaxed.

D1 defines the commit hint condition as all scopes complete plus at least one `zero_findings`, but D5 changes the executable condition to `check-approved` OK plus `review.json` existence, and D5.2 explicitly says that an all-`NotRequired::Empty` case can still emit the hint. Those statements conflict on whether a real zero-findings review is required before recommending `/track:commit`.
